I read the problem statement and the 43 prior investigations as an external proposal log. The prompt fixes the presentation format: first generators sigma,tau, implicit tame relation tau^sigma=tau^2, later generators in the pro-2 core, and exponents reduced modulo M=85667662080.The prior log leaves only one serious unrefuted candidate: the dynamic tame-inertia word from Turns 9‚Äď43; the scalar, plain-ŌÄ2, and fixed-ŌĄ‚ąí2 candidates were refuted by explicit finite-quotient tests.

The live candidate is still:variables sigma,tau,x,y definitions nx = (x*tau^50932630783)^40491355905 ny = (y*tau)^40491355905 relations x*sigma^45176306175*x*sigma^40491355905*x^-1*nx*ny^sigma*y^-1

Equivalently, with ŌÄ=ŌÄ2 and ő∑=2ŌÄ on odd tame inertia,r=xŌÉ‚ąíŌÄxŌÉŌÄx‚ąí1(xŌĄ‚ąíő∑)ŌÄ ((yŌĄ)ŌÄ)ŌÉy‚ąí1,where ŌÄ‚Č°40491355905,‚ąíŌÄ‚Č°45176306175,‚ąíő∑‚Č°50932630783(modM).The broad outside benchmark remains unchanged: the known Jannsen‚ÄďWingberg method for pÓÄ†=2 is a tame-subextension/DemuŇ°kin-formation argument, not merely a check of abstract maximal pro-p kernels; Neukirch‚Äôs report describes that explicit-determination strategy, and Jarden‚ÄďShusterman record both finite presentability and the generator/relation counts for local absolute Galois groups.Numdam+1

Audit: the two fractions must be fixed A recurring problem in the last several turns is that the sign-cover Fox coefficient was repeatedly written in both directions. The correct formula is:Da=j=0‚ąĎŌÄ‚ąí1(tőĪa)j=1‚ąítőĪa1‚ąít,where t is the sign-cover deck involution and ŌĄ‚Ü¶őĪÓÄ†=1 is a nontrivial odd tame-inertia character.

The derivation is simple but decisive:(1‚ąítőĪa)Da=1‚ąí(tőĪa)ŌÄ.

Since ŌÄ‚Č°1(mod2) and ŌÄ‚Č°0(mode), one has(tőĪa)ŌÄ=t.

HenceDa=1‚ąítőĪa1‚ąít.This is the only direction that makes the unit/radical argument valid: 1‚ąítőĪa is a 2-adic unit for őĪaÓÄ†=1, while 1‚ąít lies in the radical. Therefore Da is radical, and the coefficient contributed byx‚ąí1(xŌĄ‚ąíő∑)ŌÄis‚ąí1+D‚ąíő∑,a unit. Thus the intended conclusion survives: nontrivial odd tame-character blocks contribute only unit Smith pivots. But the formula must be written as (1‚ąít)/(1‚ąítőĪa), not its reciprocal.The trivial tame-inertia block has the analogous correction. With T the ŌÉ-cycle operator and s‚Č°ŌÄ(modf), the mod-2 linear relation is(1+T‚ąís)X+(1+T)Y=0.

If f=2a m with m odd, then s‚Č°1(mod2a) and s‚Č°0(modm). For even f, s is odd, so 1+T‚ą£1+T‚ąís.

The valid elimination is thereforeY=Ps (T)X+YňČ,Ps(T)=1+T1+T‚ąís,up to the harmless convention T‚ÜĒT‚ąí1. The reciprocal is not legitimate in F2 [T]/(Tf‚ąí1).With h(T)=1+Ps (T)+Ps(T‚ąí1),one gets, for a nontrivial f-th root ő∂,(1+ő∂)h(ő∂)=ő∂‚ąís+ő∂s+1=ő∂‚ąís(1+ő∂2s+1).Since 2s+1 is odd and congruent to 1(modm), gcd(f,2s+1)=1. Thus h has no nontrivial f-th-root zero, and h(1)=1. So the trivial block nonsingularity argument is sound with this corrected Ps.With those two formulas corrected, the prior abelianization conclusions remain consistent:Ue,fab‚ČÖZ2ef+1‚äēC2,and for the sign kernel Ve,f=kerőļ,Ve,fab‚ČÖZ22ef+1‚äēC4.These are still only consistency checks; they do not identify the full filtered tame action.

New point this turn: the mixed S3 ambiguity has an infinite tower The prior turns isolated the first mixed even-f ambiguity in the tame quotient (e,f)=(3,2), whereőĒ3,2‚ČÉS3=‚ü®ŌĄ,ŌÉ‚ą£ŌĄ3=ŌÉ2=1,¬†ŌÉŌĄŌÉ‚ąí1=ŌĄ2‚ü©.LetAi=XŌĄi,Bi=XŌĄiŌÉ (i‚ąąZ/3),and let S=ŌÉ2 be the marked unramified pro-2 element. In the reduced degree-one picture,L1‚ČÉF2[S3]‚äēF2 b‚äēF2S,and the quadratic DemuŇ°kin initial relation isq=i‚ąąZ/3‚ąĎ(Ai[2]+Bi[2])+[b,S].

DefineZd=i‚ąąZ/3‚ąĎ[Ai,Bi+d],d‚ąąZ/3.Turns 38‚Äď43 found that, after fixing a Frobenius-lift gauge, the candidate‚Äôs first mixed class is represented byőīd (b)=Zd,őīd(Ai)=[Bi+d,S],őīd(Bi+d)=[Ai,S],őīd (S)=0.This relation-preserving correction is correct: Jacobi in characteristic 2 givesőīd(q)=i‚ąĎ([Ai,[Bi+d,S]]+[Bi+d,[Ai,S]])+[Zd,S]=[Zd,S]+[Zd,S]=0.What I add here is that this is not an isolated low-level anomaly. It has the same kind of infinite filtered tower that earlier turns found in the pure unramified quadratic case.

For every n‚Č•0, setTd,n=(adZd)2n‚ąí1(S),with the convention Td,0=S. Define a higher tangent derivation byőīd,n (b)=Zd[2n],őīd,n(Ai)=[Bi+d,Td,n],őīd,n(Bi+d)=[Ai,Td,n],őīd,n (S)=0.

Then őīd,n preserves the initial DemuŇ°kin relation to first order. Indeed,őīd,n(q)=i‚ąĎ([Ai,[Bi+d,Td,n]]+[Bi+d,[Ai,Td,n]])+[Zd[2n],S].

The first sum is [Zd,Td,n], and by the restricted-Lie identity(adZd)2n=ad(Zd[2n]),we get[Zd,Td,n]=[Zd[2n],S].Thusőīd,n (q)=0 in characteristic 2.These classes are not obviously boundaries. A separating representation sends, for fixed d,Ai‚Ü¶E12 in¬†component¬†i,Bi+d‚Ü¶E21 in¬†component¬†i,inside gl2(F2)3, and sends b,S to 0. It kills q, sends Zd[2n]to (I,I,I), and kills Zd‚Ä≤ for d‚Ä≤ ÓÄ†=d. Under the C2 -operator, the image of 1+ŌÉ has zero diagonal in each matrix component, so this diagonal class cannot be removed by the usual IA-conjugacy boundary. This is a tangent-level statement, not an integration theorem, but it shows the mixed directions persist arbitrarily deep in the filtration.There is still a gauge issue: replacing the tame Frobenius lift by ŌÉ‚Ä≤=ŌĄk ŌÉ cyclesZd‚Ü¶Zd+k.

So the intrinsic object is the orbit of the three towers {őī0,n,őī1,n,őī2,n}, while the label d=0 only makes sense after fixing the same arithmetic Frobenius lift, for example the lift fixing 21/3 in L=Q2(ő∂3,21/3).This sharpens the remaining gap. Matching the arithmetic (3,2) action merely through U/U3 would not be enough; the mixed tame directions also have higher restricted-power descendants. A final dyadic formation proof has to control the full filtered operator action, not just the DemuŇ°kin kernel, orientation, sign-kernel Smith forms, or the first mixed class.

ESTABLISHED: The live dynamic candidate remains unrefuted. The correct sign-cover and trivial-block formulas areDa=1‚ąítőĪa1‚ąít,Ps(T)=1+T1+T‚ąís (up to T‚ÜĒT‚ąí1). With these directions, the prior conclusionsUe,fab‚ČÖZ2ef+1‚äēC2,(kerőļ)ab‚ČÖZ22ef+1‚äēC4 remain consistent. New this turn: in the mixed (e,f)=(3,2) quotient, the first mixed class Zd extends to an infinite family of relation-preserving tangent classes őīd,n with őīd,n (b)=Zd[2n].

GAP: This is still not a proof of the presentation of GQ2. The missing ingredient is a dyadic formation/rigidity theorem, or an explicit arithmetic comparison, proving that the candidate‚Äôs exact filtered tame action realizes the actual arithmetic Frobenius/tame-inertia action for all finite tame subextensions. The mixed S3 tower shows that low-depth checks cannot close this gap.

NEXT: Fix L=Q2(ő∂3,21/3) and the Frobenius lift fixing 21/3. Compute the arithmetic action on GL (2)/GL(2)5 first, with target őī0,0, then look one restricted-power step higher for őī0,1. If those match, formulate the general arithmetic mechanism producing the full őīd,n tower.[BREAKTHROUGH]
